style_guide.art_style: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients.
style_guide.color_palette: Deep cobalt blue, warm terracotta orange, forest green, soft ochre yellow, creamy white.
style_guide.lighting: Soft diffused light from the upper right, creating gentle gradients and warm highlights, with cool blue shadows.

characters.name: Leo
characters.description: A boy, approximately 9 years old, with curly dark brown hair, short on the sides and slightly longer on top. He has a mix of Latino and Caucasian heritage, with warm golden skin. He has large round hazel eyes and a smattering of light freckles on his nose, with noticeable dimples when he smiles. He is of average height and slightly energetic build. He wears a bright blue hooded sweatshirt (open) over an orange and white striped t-shirt, durable tan cargo shorts, and grey sneakers with orange laces. He always wears an oversized green digital watch and has a small brown leather notebook tucked into his cargo pocket.

characters.name: Dr. Aris
characters.description: A woman, in her 40s, with light olive skin tone and Middle Eastern heritage. She has dark black, shoulder-length wavy hair tied back loosely in a low bun. She has sharp, intelligent dark brown eyes and wears thin-rimmed black glasses. She is tall and athletic in build. Dr. Aris wears a navy blue tactical vest over a long-sleeved forest-green field shirt, sturdy grey utility trousers, and brown leather hiking boots. She always carries a small storm-chasing camera around her neck on a black strap.

pages.narration: The humid afternoon air felt sticky and hot, like a sweaty blanket. Have you ever felt the air before a big storm? "Look, Leo," Dr. Aris said, pointing to huge, dark clouds stretching like mountains across the sky. "A cold air mass is rushing into this warm air, making the atmosphere unstable!" The blue sky was quickly disappearing behind a wall of angry, swirling grey.
pages.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. Leo, a boy, approximately 9 years old, with curly dark brown hair, short on the sides and slightly longer on top, warm golden skin, large round hazel eyes, light freckles on his nose, and dimples when he smiles, average height and slightly energetic build, wearing a bright blue hooded sweatshirt (open) over an orange and white striped t-shirt, durable tan cargo shorts, grey sneakers with orange laces, always wearing an oversized green digital watch and has a small brown leather notebook tucked into his cargo pocket, is standing beside Dr. Aris, a woman, in her 40s, with light olive skin tone, dark black, shoulder-length wavy hair tied back loosely in a low bun, sharp, intelligent dark brown eyes, thin-rimmed black glasses, tall and athletic in build, wearing a navy blue tactical vest over a long-sleeved forest-green field shirt, sturdy grey utility trousers, and brown leather hiking boots, always carrying a small storm-chasing camera around her neck on a black strap, and they are both looking up at massive, dark, turbulent supercell clouds filling the sky over a vast, flat landscape. Soft diffused light from the upper right creates gentle gradients and warm highlights, with cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

hotspots.object: A blue jay on a fence post
A weather vane on a distant roof

visual.setting: A vast, flat landscape under massive, dark supercell clouds.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris are looking up at the turbulent storm clouds.

pages.narration: These aren't just normal storm clouds; they are part of a giant, rotating thunderstorm called a supercell! Feel that rumble in your feet? That's thunder shaking the ground! Inside this supercell, invisible forces are twisting and turning the air. Dr. Aris showed Leo how the rising warm air, called an updraft, was tilted and spun by the wind.
pages.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. Leo, a boy, approximately 9 years old, with curly dark brown hair, short on the sides and slightly longer on top, warm golden skin, large round hazel eyes, light freckles on his nose, and dimples when he smiles, average height and slightly energetic build, wearing a bright blue hooded sweatshirt (open) over an orange and white striped t-shirt, durable tan cargo shorts, grey sneakers with orange laces, always wearing an oversized green digital watch and has a small brown leather notebook tucked into his cargo pocket, and Dr. Aris, a woman, in her 40s, with light olive skin tone, dark black, shoulder-length wavy hair tied back loosely in a low bun, sharp, intelligent dark brown eyes, thin-rimmed black glasses, tall and athletic in build, wearing a navy blue tactical vest over a long-sleeved forest-green field shirt, sturdy grey utility trousers, and brown leather hiking boots, always carrying a small storm-chasing camera around her neck on a black strap, are huddled together under the intense, swirling base of a dark supercell cloud. Dr. Aris holds a device showing a simplified atmospheric cross-section diagram. Rain is starting to fall around them. Soft diffused light from the upper right creates gentle gradients and warm highlights, with cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

hotspots.object: A radar screen on Dr. Aris's device

visual.setting: Under the rotating base of a massive supercell cloud.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris are observing the storm formation. Dr. Aris holds a device.

pages.narration: The swirling cloud base seems to reach downwards, forming a scary, spinning funnel. Can you hear the low hum, getting louder by the second? It's a tornado when that rotating funnel makes contact with the ground! Leo and Dr. Aris need to decide: should they find safety immediately, or quickly watch how the wind moves first? The massive, spinning column of wind and dust is incredible and dangerous!
pages.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. Leo, a boy, approximately 9 years old, with curly dark brown hair, short on the sides and slightly longer on top, warm golden skin, large round hazel eyes, light freckles on his nose, and dimples when he smiles, average height and slightly energetic build, wearing a bright blue hooded sweatshirt (open) over an orange and white striped t-shirt, durable tan cargo shorts, grey sneakers with orange laces, always wearing an oversized green digital watch and has a small brown leather notebook tucked into his cargo pocket, and Dr. Aris, a woman, in her 40s, with light olive skin tone, dark black, shoulder-length wavy hair tied back loosely in a low bun, sharp, intelligent dark brown eyes, thin-rimmed black glasses, tall and athletic in build, wearing a navy blue tactical vest over a long-sleeved forest-green field shirt, sturdy grey utility trousers, and brown leather hiking boots, always carrying a small storm-chasing camera around her neck on a black strap, are crouching near a sturdy community building in a wide open field, pointing toward a clear funnel cloud descending from the supercell and making contact with the ground. The tornado is starting to stir up dust. Soft diffused light from the upper right creates gentle gradients and warm highlights, with cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: An open field near a sturdy community building, with a tornado forming.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris are crouching and observing the tornado touching the ground.

option_a.label: Watch the rotation (from a safe distance)
option_a.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. A detailed view of a developed tornado funnel spinning over a vast, open landscape of dry golden grasses under a massive supercell cloud. Debris is visible around the base. The illustration captures the wind patterns with layered color. Soft diffused light. Full bleed illustration.

pages.narration: Even from this safe distance, the power of the tornado is breathtaking and noisy. See the dark material at the bottom, looking like a dusty grey cloud? That's the debris ball, filled with bits of everything the tornado picks up as it spins. The wind speeds inside can be faster than a race car! A tornado's strength is rated on the Enhanced Fujita (EF) scale based on the damage it causes.

The massive funnel cloud is surrounded by a visible debris ball, swirling over a field. The scene is illuminated by soft diffused light from the upper right, casting gentle gradients. hotspots.object: A flock of cattle huddling together
A dilapidated barn roof losing shingles

visual.setting: A distant field near a fence line, watching a mature tornado.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris observe the powerful tornado from a safe distance.

pages.narration: Dr. Aris explains that scientists still have many questions about exactly how tornadoes form. Did you know a tornado over water is called a waterspout? She takes notes in her brown leather notebook, studying the tornado as it begins to thin and rope out. It's important to respect nature's power and always have a plan. Soon, the tornado lifts back into the cloud, leaving a quieter, rain-soaked landscape.
pages.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. Leo, a boy, approximately 9 years old, with curly dark brown hair, short on the sides and slightly longer on top, warm golden skin, large round hazel eyes, light freckles on his nose, and dimples when he smiles, average height and slightly energetic build, wearing a bright blue hooded sweatshirt (open) over an orange and white striped t-shirt, durable tan cargo shorts, grey sneakers with orange laces, always wearing an oversized green digital watch and has a small brown leather notebook tucked into his cargo pocket, and Dr. Aris, a woman, in her 40s, with light olive skin tone, dark black, shoulder-length wavy hair tied back loosely in a low bun, sharp, intelligent dark brown eyes, thin-rimmed black glasses, tall and athletic in build, wearing a navy blue tactical vest over a long-sleeved forest-green field shirt, sturdy grey utility trousers, and brown leather hiking boots, always carrying a small storm-chasing camera around her neck on a black strap, are standing in a rain-soaked field where the tornado just was. Leo is looking closely at Dr. Aris's brown leather notebook as she sketches a decaying tornado shape. The storm clouds are breaking up. Soft diffused light from the upper right creates gentle gradients and warm highlights, with cool blue shadows. Full bleed illustration, scene fills the entire frame edge to edge, no borders, no white margins, no frames, no vignette.

visual.setting: A rain-soaked field after the tornado has dissipated.
visual.action: Dr. Aris takes notes and Leo watches. The tornado is decaying.

option_b.label: Find immediate storm shelter
option_b.image_prompt: A textured digital illustration with visible brushwork and warm color gradients. A deep, dark concrete basement view of a sturdy community building. Overhead beams and exposed pipes are visible. Small, high windows show only dark, turbulent clouds and rain. Minimal interior lighting casts soft, deep shadows. Full bleed illustration.

pages.narration: Leo and Dr. Aris rush inside the sturdy brick community center and go straight to the basement. Where is the safest place in your home during a tornado? "The best spots are the lowest floor, a small interior room, or under the stairs, far away from any windows!" Dr. Aris tells Leo. In the dark, quiet basement, they feel safe and sound, even as the storm roars above.

hotspots.object: A stack of emergency bottled water

visual.setting: A dark, reinforced concrete basement of a community building.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris navigate the basement using a flashlight.

pages.narration: The noise outside is like a giant train passing right overhead! What do you think that sounds like? Leo holds his breath, waiting for the rumble to fade. Dr. Aris explains how meteorologists measure tornado strength by surveying the damage, using the Enhanced Fujita (EF) scale. After the storm passes and they safely step outside, they see down trees and understand why being prepared is so crucial.

The ground is wet and covered in debris and small hail. Downed tree branches are visible. The sun is breaking through the clouds. hotspots.object: A workbench with old paint cans

visual.setting: Just outside the community building entrance, seeing the storm aftermath.
visual.action: Leo and Dr. Aris step outside and view the storm damage.

pages.narration: Leo and Dr. Aris see a stunning sunset after the storm has gone, coloring the clouds pink and gold. Was that a wild experience or what? Tornadoes are one of nature's most powerful events, teaching us about air movement and the importance of safety. Leo feels excited to learn even more about weather and how to be a smart observer. "Ready for our next weather adventure, Dr. Aris?" Leo asks, his orange laces bouncing as he walks.
